claim_text: WOLF Q3 FY2026 release reported revenue $150.2M, GAAP gross margin (27)%, adjusted EBITDA ($61.7M), operating cash flow ($83.8M), liquidity $1.1648B, and Q4 revenue guide $140M-$160M.
SEC 424B3 incorporating March 29 2026 Form 10-Q confirms Chapter 11 emergence, fresh-start accounting, Q3 figures, debt/equity structure and shares outstanding context.
Wolfspeed said it emerged from Chapter 11, reduced debt about 70%, extended maturities to 2030 and lowered annual cash interest by roughly 60%.
CFIUS cleared Renesas equity issuance; Renesas receives board seat; total shares outstanding increased to about 45.1M excluding future conversions/warrants/plans.
notes: Company release plus Renesas financial report.

claim_text: Renesas says Wolfspeed shares/securities are subject to 39.9% exercise/conversion and 9.9% voting limits; Wolfspeed is not consolidated or equity-method accounted.

Wolfspeed announced $379M convertible notes plus $96.9M common/pre-funded warrants intended to redeem high-cost debt and support diversification into AI data centers, industrial and energy, aerospace and defense.

Wolfspeed closed private placement, redeemed about $475.9M senior secured notes, reduced total debt by about $97M and expected annual interest by about $62M; T. Rowe Price and Fidelity-advised accounts were cited among investors.
Wolfspeed said its next-gen TOLT portfolio targets AI datacenter power density and thermal performance, with more details on a third top-side cooled portfolio expected in second half of 2026.
notes: Product claim; commercial adoption still needs evidence.

claim_text: Wolfspeed said it produced a single-crystal 300mm SiC wafer and cites more than 2,300 issued/pending patents.
notes: Long-term commercialization and AI infrastructure claims remain forward-looking.

claim_text: Wolfspeed said it is engaging foundries, OSATs, system architects and research institutions on 300mm SiC substrates for AI/HPC packaging.
Wolfspeed introduced a commercially available 10kV SiC MOSFET for grid, industrial electrification and AI data center infrastructure.
notes: Commercial adoption still needs order/customer evidence.

claim_text: WOLF closed at $69.96 on 2026-05-14, market cap $3.15B, shares out 45.09M, 52-week range $8.05-$73.74, analyst consensus sell and target $14.33.
notes: Market data page; cross-check with exchange data if trading decision required.

claim_text: StockAnalysis news feed reported WOLF strength attributed to positive Citrini Research mention and power-chip AI narrative.
notes: Market narrative catalyst only; verify original Citrini note if accessible.

claim_text: IEA reported data center electricity demand rose 17% in 2025, AI-focused demand faster, and data center electricity consumption is set to double by 2030.
notes: Macro demand context, not company-specific validation.

claim_text: EIA forecast strongest four-year U.S. electricity demand growth since 2000, driven by large computing centers.
Reddit WOLF community was actively pushing AI/grid-power narrative after product launches and stock rally.
notes: Social sentiment only; monitor whether narrative leads to named customer/order/news.

claim_text: Retail discussion acknowledged weak quarter but focused on new products, technology leadership and survival/ramp optionality.
notes: Social sentiment only; monitor whether retail narrative shifts after Q4 guide or customer disclosures.
